segments.beat: Hand holds device close to camera against window/plant background; transparent shell clearly visible with PCB internals showing through, screen off or dim
Device rotated slightly, screen lights up with pixel game content on transparent display pane; PCB and components visible through clear enclosure
Camera pulls back slightly revealing device at natural hand-held scale against indoor window light; full device form factor visible — D-pad and A/B buttons visible on front
Slow pan/tilt around device while lit; plant and window visible in background establishing casual lifestyle context
Device held at slight angle emphasizing transparent shell depth; internal board components clearly silhouetted through resin body
Screen active with white pixel game graphics on transparent pane; hand grip shows pocket-scale size, device face-on to camera
Close macro-style shot of device face; pixel game content bright on transparent display, PCB internals visible simultaneously through shell — dual transparency payoff
Final hold on device face; clip ends

demonstrates.motion_value: Continuous slow rotation and angle shifts let viewer confirm transparency is a physical property of the shell in 3D, not a lens trick or graphic — a still from any single angle is weaker proof
Screen illuminates while PCB remains simultaneously visible through it; motion allows viewer to track that both layers coexist at multiple orientations, proving the display is genuinely transparent
Hand grip throughout video naturalistically shows true size relative to human palm; motion contextualizes it as a held object rather than a staged flat lay
Pixel game graphics visible on screen and D-pad/button layout visible on device face; no active button-pressing shown so partial rather than strong
Slow deliberate rotation and lifestyle window-light staging conveys desirable object framing; no dedicated display/shelf shot so only partial
Angled shots in natural window light catch resin surface finish and clean edges; motion highlights depth and build clarity better than a flat still

selection_note: 14s close-up hand-held loop with continuous transparency payoff — both PCB-through-shell and lit see-through display proven simultaneously across multiple angles; strongest single asset for lead hero use.

aesthetics.standout: natural window light gives a livable context
aesthetics.weakness: handheld close-up with no lock-off; device fills too little frame, constant micro-drift, screen detail unreadable
aesthetics.note: hobbyist demo footage; needs tripod, deliberate framing, and a hero angle that shows the screen legibly
